arts.ttl: Ruben Amorim gives 'simple' reason for dropping Manchester United goalkeeper Andre Onana
arts.sum: Man United take on Rangers in the Europa League on Thursday and Ruben Amorim explained his team selection ahead of the game.

arts.ttl: Man Utd admit PSR breach risk and could inflate ticket prices even further next season
arts.sum: Ticket pricing has been a hot topic for Manchester United's supporters this season and worries over Profit and Sustainability Rules have brought the situation back into focus

arts.ttl: Ruben Amorim reveals how his Man United players have responded to him branding the team 'maybe the worst in the club's history'
arts.sum: Amorim came out with the alarming comment after United suffered a seventh defeat in 15 games under their new head coach against Brighton on Sunday.

arts.ttl: Chelsea submit 'FORMAL' Garnacho offer to Man Utd
arts.sum: Chelsea have made a 'formal' offer for Alejandro Garnacho, with Manchester United now considering a big-money proposal to sell their wonderkid this month. Chelsea submit 'FORMAL' Garnacho offer to Man Utd

arts.ttl: Cork City snap up son of former Manchester United and England defender on-loan from Stoke City
arts.sum: The son of former Nottingham Forest, Arsenal, Manchester United and England defender Viv Anderson, the 18-year-old will spend the year at Turner’s Cross. Cork City have signed Stoke City teenager Freddie Anderson on loan until the end of the 2025 season.

arts.ttl: Team News: Man United head coach Ruben Amorim names forward in XI for Rangers showdown despite 'imminent' Chelsea offer
arts.sum: Manchester United head coach Ruben Amorim confirms his starting XI and bench for Thursday's vital Europa League contest with Rangers at Old Trafford.

arts.ttl: Man Utd mocked as Amad wears tracksuit with upside down badge amid club's struggles
arts.sum: Manchester United have endured a nightmare season and hit another low point this week as Amad was handed a training jacket with the club's badge printed upside-down

arts.ttl: Manchester United v Rangers: Europa League – live
arts.sum: Europa League updates from the 8pm GMT kick-off Live scores | Tables | Read Football Daily | Email Scott The battles of Britain in European football – in pictures Manchester United coach Ruben Amorim talks to TNT. “It’s always good to be back on the field … we’d like to have more practice … that would be important for the games … but this is my job … to try to help my players to win games so I am really happy to have the opportunity … the players know me … it is easier for them to recognise what I am saying than other people … I don’t control what other people think and say … but it was a little bit my fault to create all that … it is in the past … the next game is today … really important … we need to win … time to train and escape the play-offs … and have that feeling of winning at home … it is impossible to be competitive in Premier League and in Europe, especially in Europa League … it is much harder than the Champions League … not the opponents of course but the time to recover … the players have to be prepared for every game … I expect a very tough match … very British with a lot of duels … I want to see improvement … improve with the ball … be more relaxed … I will try to help them outside the pitch a little bit more.” Manchester United make five changes after the 3-1 home loss in the Premier League to Brighton & Hove Albion. Altay Bayındır, Christian Eriksen, Toby Collyer, Alejandro Garnacho and Lisandro Martínez are in for André Onana, Harry Maguire, Noussair Mazraoui, Manuel Ugarte and Kobbie Mainoo, all of whom drop to the bench. Still no involvement for Marcus Rashford. Continue reading...

arts.ttl: Man Utd might blow it AGAIN with risky January transfer
arts.sum: Obviously Man Utd need a left wing-back, but should it be Patrick Dorgu? Benfica's Alvaro Carreras who was, two years ago, a Man Utd player.

arts.ttl: Man Utd face Jadon Sancho and Jude Bellingham pain all over again as £55m ace 'responds'
arts.sum: Manchester United have had many near misses when it comes to spotting young talent, but they may be on the verge of repeating their previous mistakes by missing out on Tyler Dibling
